channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple just brought ChatGPT to iPhones, iPads, and Macs. Here’s how to make the most of it
item.description: This week, Apple rolled out its first significant update to its Apple Intelligence platform since it was launched in October.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online ‘constantly,’ Pew report finds
item.description: YouTube was the single most popular platform teenagers used — 90% said they watched videos on the site, down slightly from 95% in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Texas bill would allow bitcoin to be used for taxes, donations
item.description: Texas is among 10 states considering legislation about the expanded use of cryptocurrency by its residents
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: YouTube is taking over TV
item.description: YouTube was launched in February 2005 and soon adopted a simple motto: “Broadcast Yourself.” While we initially watched on PCs, and then smartphones, it seems that these days an increasing number of us are engaging with YouTube content on our televisions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Feds propose protection for giant salamanders devastated by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A giant salamander that evolved on the supercontinent Pangaea and outlived the dinosaurs has been proposed for federal protection by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk knocks SEC over settlement offer in probe
item.description: Tech mogul Elon Musk criticized the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday over a settlement offer in the agency’s probe into his 2022 purchase of Twitter, the social platform now known as X.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Think you're safe? Identity theft could wipe out your entire life’s savings
item.description: A former Colgate-Palmolive employee was shocked to discover $750,000 had been drained from her 401(k) account. "CyberGuy" offers tips on how to prevent identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chicago flunks cloud economics
item.description: The city of Chicago has devised a brilliant plan to jack up its cloud computing tax from 9% to 11%. As someone who has worked in tech for decades, I’ve seen this movie before, and (spoiler alert) it didn’t have a happy ending. This is not a diatribe against taxes; taxes are not the issue here.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Python AI library hack that didn’t hack Python
item.description: Guess what’s both one of the fastest-growing and most popular languages of 2024? (Hint: It’s not Java.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell
item.description: [Task Force Lima] had made so much progress and had such concrete recommendations that we moved to stand up this cell for actual execution,” said Radha Plumb. The post DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI app helps you turn anything into LEGO models
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI in the workplace is forcing younger tech workers to rethink their career paths
item.description: Entry-level workers increasingly fear job loss or significant changes to their careers due to automation made possible by generative AI (genAI).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenSilver 3.1 brings XAML designer for VS Code
item.description: OpenSilver 3.1, the latest version of Userware’s open-source framework for cross-platform .NET development, has arrived with what the company calls the first-ever drag-and-drop XAML designer for Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft to launch zero water consumption cooling for future data centers
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new design for data centers to optimize artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, implementing a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products
item.description: A provision that made it into the compromise legislation will give the Defense Department components more authority to purchase alternative cyber products. The post NDAA to give DoD components more flexibility to procure cyber products first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to build better AI chatbots
item.description: At Campfire, we launched a consumer AI chat product called Cozy Friends on Steam and mobile on October 12. Imagine Animal Crossing or The Sims with AI agents. In the first 30 days, users exchanged more than 1.7M messages with our AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China launches anti-monopoly probe into Nvidia amid rising US-China chip tensions
item.description: China has initiated an investigation into Nvidia over alleged violations of the country’s anti-monopoly laws, signaling a potential escalation in the ongoing tech and trade tensions between Beijing and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Protecting yourself from Microsoft tech support scams
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says scammers exploit fears, falsely claim computer issues to access your personal data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
